title: Are we being deceived about grass-finished labeling?

chunks.title: Introduction to Meat Stick Ingredients
chunks.summary: The video starts with a detailed overview of the ingredients in their grass-fed, regenerative meat stick, highlighting the natural preservatives used, such as vinegar, and the absence of additives. The creators emphasize their commitment to quality and simplicity, ensuring a great taste without compromising on standards.

chunks.title: Grass-Fed vs. Conventional Meat
chunks.summary: A discussion unfolds about the difference between genuine grass-fed meat and cheaper alternatives often available in stores. The hosts explain that many products labeled as grass-fed may actually be derived from animals fed synthetic pellets, which undermines the quality consumers expect.

chunks.title: Transparency in Sourcing
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the challenges of sourcing high-quality meat. The hosts express frustration over suppliers who refuse to provide transparency about their practices or allow farm visits, underscoring the importance of knowing the source of ingredients to maintain high standards.
chunks.transcript: stick like this and this is where the issue of transparency comes in and why we want to hold this so closely in our values with this company is because we want to show what under the hood in our meat stick so we want to show the nutrients the micronutrients the absence of these synthetic vitamins because we're not using Farms that are going to feed them pellets the word that I've heard used is greenwashing and I think it's a little uh a little bit uh uh confusing for people it basically means you're getting grass pellet fed cows that are called grass-fed and not actual grass on a Green Farm fed cows there are so many farms that Josh and I chatted with asking for two things one is do you have information about your practices two is can we visit the farm and the amount of knows that we got or you can know once you get to a certain volume of purchasing like what how does that make any sense like I have to buy from you and then sell the product to Consumers before I know what's actually in the product that you're giving me it was about a 90% no when to grassfed suppliers where we ask can we visit the farm do you have a spec on like an affidavit that you'll assign that says you do these practices no no no no no no no no no

chunks.title: Production Techniques for Quality
chunks.summary: The hosts elaborate on the innovative production methods used to create the meat stick, focusing on air drying over cooking or curing. They explain how their proprietary technique helps retain nutrients and eliminates the need for artificial preservatives, while maintaining product integrity.

chunks.title: Health Benefits and Personal Experiences
chunks.summary: Lastly, the discussion highlights the health benefits experienced by the hosts when consuming their meat stick, noting an increase in energy and overall well-being. They share testimonials from others who have enjoyed the product, reinforcing its effectiveness and quality.
